title: sidalcea neomexicana
intro: Sidalcea neomexicana, also known as New Mexico checkerbloom, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Native American medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including colds, fever, and sore throats. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which provide several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Sidalcea neomexicana can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal preparations such as salves and ointments to aid in wound healing and skin issues.
